task.title: Changing the high-level qualifier of an MVS subproject
task.shortdesc: You can change the high-level qualifier (HLQ) of an MVS subproject after it is created.

taskbody.context: When you create an MVS subproject, you can choose a high-level qualifier for the subproject. The default HLQ is the user ID with which you are logged on to the remote system when you create a project. This HLQ is used to resolve the <HLQ> substitution variable that is displayed in some property values associated with resources contained by the subproject. You can see the HLQ associated with a subproject by selecting the subproject and opening the Properties view.

steps.stepsection: To change the HLQ associated with a subproject:

step.cmd: In the view, select the subproject.
Click Info to open the Info page.
Select a new HLQ from the High-level Qualifier list.
Click OK to save the change.
step.info: The product obtains the HLQs for this list from the filters that are defined under the MVS Files node in the Remote Systems view.
